what_it_does: A read-only reference-verification audit that compares the Concurrent Cognition triad plan against the actual source in ~/company-cognition, recording which claims match, which are partially missed, and which line citations are stale.
observations: It opens with a one-line verdict that the plan architecture and net-new ledger are accurate.
It groups findings into three categories: substantive mismatches, citation drift, and confirmed-accurate claims.
It identifies an existing MODEL_KNOBS/knobs_for capability surface that the plan's MODEL_CAPABILITIES claim appears to overlook.
It flags a conflict between the plan's mark llm VOLATILE suggestion and the existing memoization-as-feature behavior.
It notes the scheduler currently lacks a materialized ready-set, so concurrent dispatch would require new structure.
It reports a consistent ~+14 line offset across runtime/suite.py citations.
It includes tables mapping plan claims to real file locations and a section of unverified or out-of-scope items.
It closes with a summary list of key inaccuracies.
standouts: The audit finds the plan's net-new ledger is accurate but misses an existing capability resolver (MODEL_KNOBS/knobs_for).
It argues the mark llm VOLATILE recommendation would break documented memo reuse and should be replaced by a per-draw cache salt.
It points out that the scheduler's ready-set is a conceptual structure not yet present in code.
It warns that line-number drift is harmless to humans but hazardous for an automated build agent that seeks by line.
conventions: Uses a claim/reality/holds/action block structure for each finding.
Groups findings into substantive, citation-drift, and confirmed-accurate categories.
Relies heavily on file:line citations and symbol names.
Mixes verification with prescriptive corrective actions.
concerns: The file embeds evaluative and prescriptive language inside a verification artifact.
It depends on line numbers that it simultaneously reports as stale, which could confuse automated consumers.
It references external source files without including their contents, so readers must trust the cited locations.
It combines structural findings, cosmetic citation drift, and confirmed-accurate claims in one document.
notes: A code-vs-plan audit that largely validates the Concurrent Cognition design while flagging three substantive mismatches and a batch of stale line numbers.
questions: What exact commit or timestamp of ~/company-cognition was inspected?
How were the cited line numbers generated, and when will they be refreshed?
Which of the proposed corrective actions are already accepted as plan amendments?
What defines the boundary between substantive and citation-drift categories?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches most of the content, though the file also prescribes corrective actions and groups findings by category, going slightly beyond pure verification.
apparent_themes: Preservation of existing behavior such as memoization
intention_signals: To harden the Concurrent Cognition build plan by grounding it in the actual codebase, preventing an automated build agent from trusting stale citations or reimplementing existing machinery, and steering the plan toward corrections before implementation.
novelty: It is a meta-review of a design triad against real code that explicitly quantifies line-citation drift and calls out the automated build agent as the intended consumer of that precision.
contribution: It contributes a verified mapping between Concurrent Cognition plan claims and existing source locations, a catalog of which components are net-new versus already present, and reconciliation notes that help align the plan with the codebase.
summary_for_embedding: A reference-verification review of the Concurrent Cognition triad against the company-cognition source, recording claim accuracy, net-new versus existing components, substantive mismatches, and line-citation drift.
intention_for_embedding: To validate the Concurrent Cognition implementation plan against the real codebase before automated build execution and surface needed corrections.
